Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are asked to analyze an event log from a Procure to Pay P2P process We need to identify anomalies in the sequences relative to standard P2P process and explain why they are problematic

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard P2P process flow typically follows this sequence 1 Create Purchase Requisition PR 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Issue Purchase Order PO 4 Receive Goods or services 5 Vendor Invoice Received 6 Match Invoice to PO and goods receipt 3 way match 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case maybe after payment

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Some variations sometimes invoice may arrive before goods but goods must be received before payment is released or at least invoice matched to PO Also release payment typically occurs after successful match Approve PR must happen before issuing PO Receiving goods normally happens after PO is issued but could happen before if process is not followed though thats anomalous In some cases invoice may be received before goods but payment should not be released until goods are received and matched

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case This follows standard flow PR approved before PO goods received after PO invoice after goods match payment close Looks normal

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Create PR Issue Purchase Order no approval before PO Thats a problem Purchase requisition should be approved before issuing a PO Vendor Invoice Received Release Payment payment released before goods received before match and even before PR approval Highly anomalous Approve Purchase Requisition approval after PO and after payment released Receive Goods Match Invoice to PO match done after payment Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So multiple anomalies PO issued without PR approval payment released before goods receipt invoice match and even PR approval match after payment This violates segregation of duties authorization controls and three way match principle Risk of paying for goods not received or not ordered properly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment payment released before vendor invoice received before goods received before match Vendor Invoice Received after payment Receive Goods after payment Match Invoice to PO after payment Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies Payment released before invoice receipt goods receipt and match This is a major control failure Payment should be the last step after successful match

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods goods received before PO issued Issue Purchase Order PO issued after goods received Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly Goods received before PO is issued In standard P2P a PO is a commitment to buy goods should not be accepted without a PO This could indicate unauthorized procurement or a process where goods are delivered without proper purchase order leading to maverick buying approval bypass and invoice matching issues though they did match later Its a control weakness

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So all cases except 1001 show anomalies Case 1002 and 1003 have payment before goods receipt and match Case 1002 also lacks PR approval before PO Case 1004 has goods receipt before PO

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should structure the explanation identify each case the anomalous sequence why its problematic control risks fraud risk operational inefficiencies compliance issues
